prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye ask why the sky be so blue, ye scurvy dog? Well, lend me yer lugholes and I’ll tell ye true!

It be the work o’ the sun’s rays dancin’ with the air, that’s what! The sky be filled with tiny, near-invisible particles—like ghostly dust floatin’ on the wind. Now, when Old Sol casts his light o’er the briny deep, his beams do split as they pass through this airy mist.

Blue light, see, be the feisty sort—short and quick, it scatters ‘round more than the other hues, like a cutlass in a tavern brawl! Red and yellow be sluggish, ploddin’ straight on, but blue—it leaps and flits in every direction, paintin’ the whole dome o’ heaven in a bold, bilgeless blue!

So when ye gaze aloft, ‘tis this scattered blue light that fills yer eyes, lad! ‘Tis nature’s own broadside fired across the heavens, and I’d wager me last doubloon it’s fairer than any treasure-laden galleon.

Now, if ye’ll excuse me, I’ve a rendezvous with a storm and a bottle o’ rum—both be equally tempestuous!
